Assignment 3: Demo Walkthrough 8 annotated screenshots · n8n workflow running end-to-end · 271 records from 4 source types INFO 7375 Branding & AI | Vaibhav Singh | May 29, 2026 | Madison Intelligence Agent Metric Value Clean records 271 (274 raw, 3 duplicates removed, 0 invalid) Source types 4: Tech RSS (50), Google News RSS (100), NewsAPI (80), HuggingFace (44) Quality rate 98.9% Output 115 kB CSV · 271 rows × 9 columns Workflow 18 nodes: trigger + 6 sources + 5 normalizers + 5 merges + dedup + csv + save Screenshot 01: Full Workflow Overview — All Nodes Green 18-node workflow executing end-to-end. 6 sources fan out from trigger, each feeds a normalizer. Merge chain accumulates 40→50→150→230→274, then Dedup outputs 271 clean records as 115 kB CSV. ① Green circle: Manual trigger fires all 6 source branches in parallel ② Amber zone: 6 source nodes (3 RSS + Google News + NewsAPI + HuggingFace) ③ Blue zone: 5 normalizer Code nodes standardize to 9-column schema ④ Green zone: Merge chain — each node waits for both inputs before passing forward ⑤ Red zone: Dedup (274→271) → Convert to CSV → Save (115 kB) Screenshot 02: Source: RSS — TechCrunch (20 articles) RSS Feed Read node with TechCrunch feed URL. No authentication. Output shows 20 recent articles. ① Feed URL: https://techcrunch.com/feed/ — zero config, just the URL ② 20 articles with creator, title, link — Blue Origin explosion, Glean $300M, Waymo registrations Screenshot 03: Source: RSS — Google News Brand Search (100 articles) Google News RSS search for brand reputation + brand monitoring + brand voice AI. 100 articles from diverse publishers. ① Search query encoded in RSS URL — brand monitoring keywords ② 100 items — brand-focused: 'Writer helps solve brand consistency', 'AI Brand Governance Crisis' Screenshot 04: Source: HTTP — NewsAPI (80 articles from 702 results) NewsAPI /v2/everything with brand monitoring query. Key fix: Authentication = None, API key as query parameter (not Bearer). ① Authentication set to None — Bearer auth causes 401 errors with NewsAPI ② API key passed as query parameter alongside q, language, sortBy, pageSize ③ 702 total results, 80 fetched — articles from Digiday, Screen Rant, CBC News Screenshot 05: Normalizer: Raw RSS Input → JavaScript Code → Standardized 9-Column Output Three-panel view showing the transformation. Left = raw RSS fields. Center = normalizer logic. Right = clean schema output. ① Raw input: creator, title, link, pubDate, contentSnippet, categories — inconsistent across feeds ② JavaScript: date parsing, topic classification (regex), HTML stripping, reliability scoring ③ Clean output: id, title, source_name, source_url, published_date (YYYY-MM-DD), topic_category, content_snippet, reliability_score, collection_method Screenshot 06: Merge Chain: Accumulating All Sources (→ 230 items) Merge +NewsAPI receives 150 accumulated items on Input 1 and 80 NewsAPI items on Input 2. Append mode waits for both. ① Input 1: 150 items accumulated (TC:20 + Ars:20 + Verge:10 + GNews:100) ② Input 2: 80 items from NewsAPI normalizer ③ Output: 230 combined items, all with consistent 9-column schema Screenshot 07: Deduplicate & Validate: Quality Gate (274 → 271) Code node validates every record: checks required fields, enforces YYYY-MM-DD dates, removes title-based duplicates. 3 caught. ① Input: 274 items from Merge +HF (all 6 sources combined) ② Validation code: title dedup (case-insensitive, first 60 chars), date regex, empty-field rejection ③ Output: 271 clean records across 11 pages — 3 cross-source duplicates removed, 0 invalid Screenshot 08: Final Output: 115 kB CSV — Download Ready Binary CSV file visible on both input and output panels. Click Download to save as Singh_Vaibhav_A3_Data.csv. ① Binary file info: File.csv, 115 kB, text/csv MIME type ② Download button — saves the submission CSV file ③ Output panel confirms successful file processing Pipeline Summary This workflow collects 271 clean, deduplicated records from 4 source types into a 115 kB CSV. Every record has 9 standardized fields. The merge chain guarantees all sources complete before deduplication. Ready for Assignment 4: sentiment classification, entity extraction, and brand-health report generation. Vaibhav Singh | singh.vaibha@northeastern.edu | github.com/vaibhavsingh97

Assignment 3: Data Collection Documentation INFO 7375 Branding & AI | Vaibhav Singh | May 29, 2026 | Madison Intelligence Agent 1. Data Inventory Project: Madison Intelligence Agent (Brand Reputation Monitor) Problem: Marketing teams lack an automated, open-source way to monitor brand voice consistency across news, social, and review channels. This data collection pipeline gathers the raw text data the Intelligence Agent needs to classify sentiment, extract entities, and generate brand-health reports. Source 1: News RSS Feeds (TechCrunch, Ars Technica, The Verge) Field Detail Type Tech news articles from major outlet RSS feeds Records 50 records (TechCrunch: 20, Ars Technica: 20, The Verge: 10) Purpose Primary brand-monitoring corpus: real articles mentioning companies, products, and technologies that the Intelligence Agent will classify for sentiment and entity extraction. Method Three n8n RSS Feed Read nodes, each feeding a dedicated Code normalizer node. No API key required. Each normalizer standardizes titles, dates (YYYY-MM-DD), and content snippets. Quality 100% complete — all 50 records have all 9 fields populated. RSS feeds are well-structured with reliable date parsing. Reliability Score 4/5 (established tech journalism outlets with editorial standards) Source 2: Google News RSS (brand monitoring search queries) Field Detail Type Brand-focused news articles from Google News RSS search feed Records 100 records — the largest single source in the pipeline Purpose Brand-specific content: articles about brand reputation, brand monitoring, and brand voice consistency from diverse outlets. These are the domain-relevant records the agent needs to understand brand health language. Google News aggregates from hundreds of publishers, giving source diversity that a single-outlet RSS feed cannot. Method n8n RSS Feed Read node pointed at Google News RSS search URL: news.google.com/rss/search?q=brand+reputation+OR+brand+monitoring+OR+brand+voice+AI. No API key required. Normalizer extracts source name from title suffix (e.g. 'Article Title - Reuters'). Quality ~95% complete. Google News titles reliably include the source outlet name. Content snippets are shorter than direct-feed RSS (title-only for some), so content_snippet falls back to the article title when the feed doesn't include a summary. Reliability Score 3-5 (varies by original outlet — Reuters/Bloomberg = 5, TechCrunch/Forbes = 4, unknown blogs = 3) Source 3: HuggingFace 20_newsgroups (benchmark dataset for eval) Field Detail Type Labeled text classification dataset from HuggingFace Hub (SetFit/20_newsgroups) Records 44 records (filtered from 80 fetched rows to relevant categories: technology, security, business) Field Detail Purpose Evaluation benchmark data. These records have known ground-truth topic labels, so the Intelligence Agent's topic classifier can be scored against labeled data. This is the golden dataset foundation for the eval harness described in Assignment 2. The 36 excluded records were in irrelevant categories (sports, religion, politics) that don't apply to brand monitoring. Method n8n HTTP Request node to HuggingFace Datasets Server API (datasets-server.huggingface.co/rows). No API key required. Code normalizer filters by category and maps newsgroup labels to the pipeline's topic taxonomy. Quality 100% complete (structured dataset with known labels). Content is older Usenet posts, not modern news, so topic labels are the primary value rather than text style. All 44 records have ground-truth topic_category labels. Reliability Score 3/5 (academic benchmark dataset, not live news) Summary Statistics (from actual workflow run — May 28, 2026) Metric Value Total raw records 194 (before deduplication) Clean records after dedup 192 Duplicates removed 2 (cross-source overlap between RSS feeds and Google News) Invalid records skipped 0 Quality rate 98.97% (192 / 194) Complete records (all 9 fields) 192 / 192 (100%) File size 82 kB (CSV) Sources 3 source types: RSS Feeds (50), Google News RSS (100), HuggingFace (44) Collection methods 3 (rss_feed, google_news_rss, huggingface_dataset) Date format YYYY-MM-DD — validated by regex in dedup node Ready for Assignment 4: Agent classification, entity extraction, report generation Pipeline Flow (all nodes green) Node Items Role Run Workflow 1 trigger Manual trigger — kicks off all 5 source branches in parallel RSS — TechCrunch 20 Fetch latest 20 articles from techcrunch.com/feed/ RSS — Ars Technica 20 Fetch latest 20 articles from Ars Technica RSS RSS — The Verge 10 Fetch latest 10 articles from The Verge RSS RSS — Google News 100 Search Google News RSS for brand reputation + monitoring + AI HTTP — HuggingFace 1 (JSON) Fetch 80 rows from 20_newsgroups via Datasets Server API Norm TechCrunch 20 Standardize fields, classify topic, set reliability=4 Norm Ars Technica 20 Standardize fields, classify topic, set reliability=4 Norm The Verge 10 Standardize fields, classify topic, set reliability=4 Norm Google News 100 Extract source name from title, variable reliability scoring Norm HuggingFace 44 Filter to relevant categories, map labels to taxonomy Node Items Role Merge TC+Ars 40 Append TechCrunch + Ars Technica items Merge +Verge 50 Append + The Verge items Merge +GNews 150 Append + Google News items Merge +HF 194 Append + HuggingFace items — all sources merged Deduplicate & Validate 192 Remove 2 dupes, validate dates + required fields Convert to CSV 192 Convert JSON items to CSV binary (82 kB) Save CSV 1 file Write to disk / download 2. Setup Guide n8n visual workflow (primary collection method). No API keys required — all sources are public. Step Action Notes 1 Install n8n npm install -g n8n (requires Node.js 18+) 2 Start n8n n8n start — then open http://localhost:5678 3 Import workflow Three-dot menu (top right) > Import from File > select Singh_Vaibhav_A3_Workflow.json 4 Execute workflow Click play button (top right). All 5 source branches fire in parallel. Watch merge nodes accumulate: 40 → 50 → 150 → 194 → 192 clean. 5 Download CSV Click Convert to CSV node > left panel shows 'data' binary (82 kB, text/csv) > click Download button. Save as Singh_Vaibhav_A3_Data.csv. 6 Export workflow Three-dot menu > Export > saves .json for Canvas submission Required Credentials Credential Required ? How to Get Cost Google News RSS No Public RSS URL, no authentication or signup. Free, unlimited HuggingFace token Yes Sign up at huggingface.co. Go to Settings > Access Tokens > create a read token. Paste it as a Bearer token header in the HTTP node. Free RSS feeds No TechCrunch, Ars Technica, The Verge — all public URLs. Free Common Errors and Fixes Error Cause Fix RSS feed returns empty Feed temporarily down Workflow continues with other sources via merge chain. Check feed URL in browser. HuggingFace 429 rate limit Too many requests in short window Wait 60 seconds and re-run. The API throttles burst requests but is generous overall. n8n won't start Port 5678 already in use Run: lsof -i :5678 then kill the process. Or: n8n start --port 5679 Save CSV 'not writable' n8n restricts filesystem access by default Click the Convert to CSV node and use the Download button on the binary output panel instead. Or restart n8n with: N8N_RESTRICT_FILE_ACCESS_TO="/Users/" n8n start Error Cause Fix Convert to CSV shows 0 items Multi-input Code node timing issue in older n8n versions Use the merge chain architecture (current workflow) — each Merge node has exactly 2 inputs and waits for both before passing data forward. 3. Data Quality Report Quality Checklist Check Status Evidence 80%+ records complete PASS 100% of 192 records have all 9 fields populated. Validated in the Deduplicate & Validate code node — any record missing title, published_date, or source_name is rejected before output. Essential fields present PASS title, published_date, source_name present in 192/192 records (100%). Enforced by code-level validation, not just schema — the dedup node checks each field explicitly. No duplicate entries PASS Title-based deduplication removed 2 duplicates from 194 raw records (cross-source overlap between TechCrunch RSS and Google News). Dedup uses case-insensitive first-60-chars matching. Dates in YYYY-MM-DD PASS All 192 records use YYYY-MM-DD format. The normalizer nodes parse multiple input formats (RFC 2822, ISO 8601, etc.) and standardize to YYYY-MM-DD. The dedup node rejects any record where the date doesn't match the regex /^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/. Data relevant to problem PASS RSS feeds (50 records) provide brand/tech news directly relevant to brand monitoring. Google News (100 records) was searched specifically for 'brand reputation OR brand monitoring OR brand voice AI'. HuggingFace (44 records) provides labeled benchmark data for the eval harness. Data Schema Column Type Example Validation id str rss-tc-1 Unique prefix per source + counter title str Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket explodes during testing in Florida Max 150 chars, HTML stripped, required source_name str TechCrunch Required. Used for reliability scoring. source_url str https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/28/bl ue-origins-... Full URL to original article published_date str 2026-05-29 YYYY-MM-DD enforced by regex. Invalid = rejected. topic_category str technology ai_ml, brand_marketing, security, business, big_tech, technology content_snippet str The explosion is likely a major setback for Jeff Bezos'... First 200 chars, HTML stripped. Falls back to title. reliability_score int 4 1-5 scale. 5=Reuters. 4=TechCrunch. 3=unknown. collection_meth od str rss_feed rss_feed, google_news_rss, huggingface_dataset Error Handling The workflow handles source failures gracefully via the merge chain architecture. Each source branch runs independently. If one RSS feed is temporarily unavailable, the other 4 sources still produce data. The chain of Merge nodes (TC+Ars → +Verge → +GNews → +HF) waits for both inputs at each stage before passing data forward, ensuring no branch is skipped due

sample_rows.content_snippet: The explosion is likely a major setback for Jeff Bezos' spaceflight company, and its attempt to compete with SpaceX.
sample_rows.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket explodes during testing in Florida

sample_rows.content_snippet: The enterprise AI search startup tripled its annual revenue even as tech giants entered the category.
sample_rows.title: Glean’s top line crosses $300M as AI budget-cutting becomes its major selling point

sample_rows.content_snippet: A new law and AV tracker tool gives the clearest accounting yet of how many robotaxis and self-driving trucks are in Texas.
sample_rows.title: Waymo dominates autonomous vehicle registrations as Tesla trails behind
